name: Inverted Dependency Chain
summary: Inverted Dependency Chain

name: Tau-Admissible Point
summary: Tau-Admissible Point

name: Constraint Lattice
summary: The five constraints (prime, non-negativity, remainder, primorial, depth) on ABCD quadruples that define tau-admissible points. The conjunction forms a lattice whose meet is tau-admissibility.

name: Point Set Well-Defined
summary: The tau-admissible point set is well-defined: the five constraint conditions (prime, non-negativity, remainder, primorial, depth) are jointly satisfiable for all primes from 2 to 20, producing a non-empty finite set at each stage.

name: Omega Readout
summary: The map Phi_omega that assigns to each path approaching omega its base limit and its fiber limit on L. Extracts the B/C fiber dominance from paths of unbounded primorial depth.

name: Fiber Degeneration at Omega
summary: At the omega boundary, the fiber T^2 degenerates: paths of unbounded primorial depth produce balanced B/C ratios, forcing the fiber to collapse onto the lemniscate L = S^1 v S^1.

name: Lemniscate as Coordinate Limit
summary: Lemniscate as Coordinate Limit

name: Base tau^1
summary: The base of the tau^3 fibration: the two-dimensional space tau^1 = {(D, A)} where A is a tau-prime and prime factors of D are strictly less than A. Carries the radial and address coordinates.

name: Fiber T^2
summary: The fiber of the tau^3 fibration: the two-dimensional space T^2 = {(B, C)} where B records the exponent (gamma-orbit) and C records the tetration height (eta-orbit). Carries the solenoidal coordinates.

name: Fibered Product tau^3
summary: Fibered Product tau^3

name: Fibration Structure
summary: The fibered product tau^3 = tau^1 x_f T^2 is a non-trivial fibration: surjective projection, varying fiber, no global trivialization, and injective ABCD chart.

name: Interior Bipolar Decomposition
summary: Interior Bipolar Decomposition

name: Sector Inheritance
summary: Every tau-admissible point inherits a bipolar sector assignment from the boundary structure via fiber coordinates, compatible with the idempotent decomposition of H_tau.

name: ABCD vs Quaternions
summary: Comparison of the quaternionic approach (dim 4, noncommutative, no zero divisors, imported) with the ABCD approach (commutative, bipolar zero divisors, earned from axioms). Quaternionic structure is incompatible with tau's bipolar boundary.

name: Four-Ray Rigidity
summary: The four ABCD rays provide complete coordinates, bipolar compatibility, and coherence constraints through the fibered product structure, establishing holomorphic rigidity without importing quaternionic algebra.

name: Cylinder Domain
summary: A subset of tau^3 expressible as a finite Boolean combination of stage-k cylinders. The cylinder domains form the Boolean algebra generated by {C_k(x)}.

name: Stage-k Cylinder
summary: The set C_k(x) of all points agreeing with x modulo the k-th primorial P_k. Equivalently, the preimage of the stage-k reduction map at x.

name: Clopen Basis
summary: The collection B = {C_k(x)} of all stage-k cylinders. Every element is clopen: both C_k(x) and its complement are finite unions of basis elements.

name: Cylinder Basis Theorem
summary: The collection of stage-k cylinders forms a basis for the cylinder topology on tau^3: they are clopen, nested by divisibility, and any two cylinders are either disjoint or nested.

name: First Disagreement Depth
summary: First Disagreement Depth

name: Ultrametric Distance
summary: Ultrametric Distance

name: Ultrametric Triangle Inequality
summary: Ultrametric Triangle Inequality

name: Cylinders Are Balls
summary: Stage-k cylinders equal both closed and open ultrametric balls, so cylinders and balls are identical objects in the cylinder topology.

name: Holomorphic Implies Continuous
summary: Holomorphic Implies Continuous

name: Naturality Forces Cylinder Compatibility
summary: Naturality Forces Cylinder Compatibility

name: Topological Skeleton
summary: Topological Skeleton

name: Stone Space
summary: A topological space that is compact, Hausdorff, and totally disconnected; equivalently, a profinite space. tau^3 is a Stone space by II.T07-T09, with clopen basis from the cylinder topology.

name: Compactness
summary: The topological space tau^3 with the cylinder topology is compact, via Tychonoff's theorem applied to the inverse limit of finite discrete spaces.

name: Hausdorff Property
summary: tau^3 is Hausdorff: any two distinct points are separated by disjoint cylinder neighborhoods at the first disagreement depth.

name: Total Disconnectedness
summary: Total Disconnectedness

name: Topology Uniqueness
summary: The profinite topology on tau^3 is the unique topology satisfying CRT continuity, Hausdorff separation, and compactness.

name: Topology as Invariant
summary: Topology as Invariant

name: Tau-Dimension
summary: The minimal number of independent refinement rays whose joint cylinders separate all points of tau^3. Replaces classical covering dimension (which yields zero for profinite spaces).

name: Dimension Four
summary: The topological dimension of tau is exactly 4: four ABCD rays jointly separate all points (upper bound), pairwise independence shows no triple suffices (lower bound).

name: Radial-Solenoidal Split
summary: Radial-Solenoidal Split

name: Boundary Minimality
summary: The boundary lemniscate L = S^1 v S^1 is the minimal quotient of T^2 preserving both U(1) gauge factors, reducing codimension, with unique crossing point.

name: Angular Sectors
summary: Clopen subsets of tau^3 defined by B- or C-coordinate constraints at a fixed stage k. B-sectors fix the exponent mod p_k; C-sectors fix the tetration mod p_k. The two are independent.

name: Lobes as Clopen Sets
summary: Lobes as Clopen Sets

name: Torus Degeneration Theorem
summary: Torus Degeneration Theorem

name: Pinch Map
summary: The surjection p: T^2 -> L collapsing the diagonal circle to the wedge point. The canonical degeneration of torus to lemniscate.

name: Fundamental Group Degeneration
summary: Fundamental Group Degeneration

name: Two-Readout Principle
summary: Two-Readout Principle: topology (fine-grain) and geometry (coarse-grain) are parallel readouts of the coherence kernel

name: Spine Address Path
summary: Spine Address Path: canonical address route between finite-stage points via alpha_1

name: Refinement Rays
summary: Refinement Rays: one-sided N-like canonical iterations via rho providing ABCD coordinate structure

name: Compatibility Axiom Schema
summary: Compatibility Axiom Schema: geometric constructions must be decidable and coherence-respecting

name: Betweenness Relation
summary: Betweenness Relation

name: Betweenness Axioms
summary: The betweenness relation from the ultrametric distance satisfies all three Tarski betweenness axioms (identity, transitivity, connectivity).

name: Congruence Relation
summary: Segment congruence on tau^3 defined by AB ~ CD iff d(A,B) = d(C,D), where d is the canonical ultrametric distance.

name: Congruence Axioms
summary: The ultrametric congruence relation satisfies all six Tarski congruence axioms (reflexivity, identity, transitivity, segment construction, five-segment, inner transitivity).

name: Pasch Axiom
summary: The Pasch axiom holds in tau^3: the proof reduces to combinatorial cylinder containment via the ultrametric isosceles property.

name: Parallel Postulate
summary: The parallel postulate holds in tau^3: for any line l and point p not on l, there exists a unique parallel through p, constructed via CRT product of prime-order affine spaces.

name: Wave-Type PDE
summary: The split-complex Cauchy-Riemann equations yield the hyperbolic wave equation. The PDE is hyperbolic (not elliptic), with two families of real characteristics.

name: Causal Structure
summary: The partial order on null-line segments of tau^3 determined by the two characteristic families from the wave equation and the forward direction from B/C asymmetry (Prime Polarity, I.T05).

name: Euclidean as Static Limit
summary: Euclidean as Static Limit

name: Orthodox Denotation Bridge
summary: Orthodox Denotation Bridge

name: Approximation Sequence
summary: Approximation Sequence

name: Alpha-Ray Line
summary: A line in tau^3 along the alpha-ray direction, defined using the prime-indexed coordinate A and the ultrametric distance. The one-dimensional subspace traced by varying the address coordinate.

name: Level Circle
summary: The finite cyclic group at NF stage k in each angular direction. The finite-stage circle whose inverse limit yields the solenoidal circle.

name: R as Inverse Limit
summary: The real numbers are recovered as the Archimedean inverse limit of primorial-scaled residues, with each coherent sequence determining a unique real via nested interval intersection.

name: Solenoidal Circle
summary: The inverse limit of finite cyclic groups in each angular direction. A compact, totally disconnected, torsion-free profinite group whose Archimedean projection is S^1.

name: S^1 as Profinite Limit
summary: S^1 as Profinite Limit

name: Geometric-Topological Unification
summary: Geometric-Topological Unification

name: Geometric Pi
summary: The circumference-to-diameter ratio of the earned circle S^1, defined as the common limit of inscribed and circumscribed Archimedes polygon perimeters normalized to unit diameter.

name: Archimedes Polygon Sequence
summary: Archimedes Polygon Sequence

name: Three Perspectives on Pi
summary: Pi is earned from within tau via three equivalent perspectives: geometric (circumference/diameter of the earned circle), analytic (Leibniz series from the tau-harmonic expansion), and algebraic (eigenvalue of angular iteration on T^2).

name: e as Iterator Eigenvalue
summary: e as Iterator Eigenvalue

name: Growth Base
summary: The unique positive real number b satisfying the self-reproduction condition with log b = 1. Characterizes e as the growth rate eigenvalue of the nu-iterator.

name: e from Index Arithmetic
summary: Euler's number e is earned from within tau via index arithmetic: it is the unique growth rate eigenvalue of the nu-iterator, with three equivalent characterizations (factorial series, compound limit, index-arithmetic trace).

name: Interior j-Unit
summary: The split-complex unit j: the automorphism of the fiber T^2 exchanging B and C coordinates, with j^2 = +1. Unlike i (rotation), j is an involution (polarity/reflection).

name: Bipolar Idempotents Interior
summary: Bipolar Idempotents Interior

name: j Replaces i
summary: The fiber involution sigma: B <-> C is the unique non-trivial ABCD-preserving automorphism of T^2, forcing j (with j^2 = +1) as the canonical algebraic structure. The classical i has no tau-internal avatar.

name: Polarity vs Rotation
summary: Polarity vs Rotation

name: Master Constant Confirmed
summary: Geometric confirmation that iota_tau = 2/(pi + e) is the unique coupling between the earned angular constant pi (II.T22) and the earned growth constant e (II.T23). It mediates between B/C channels and bridges ultrametric refinement to Archimedean resolution.

name: Archimedean Bridge
summary: The map connecting the ultrametric profinite structure of tau^3 to Archimedean real-valued measurements, via iota_tau = 2/(pi + e). Bridges finite-stage computation to real analysis.

name: Refinement Equals Resolution
summary: Refinement Equals Resolution

name: Refinement Resolution Bound
summary: Refinement Resolution Bound

name: Calibrated Split-Complex Codomain
summary: Calibrated Split-Complex Codomain

name: Geometric Meaning of Bipolar Idempotents
summary: Geometric Meaning of Bipolar Idempotents

name: BndLift Construction
summary: BndLift Construction

name: BndLift Existence
summary: For every stage n and holomorphic datum, the boundary lift exists and is the unique extension to stage n+1 satisfying tower coherence and diagonal discipline.

name: Bipolar Channel Independence
summary: Bipolar Channel Independence

name: Refinement-Spectral Equivalence
summary: Refinement sequence data (stage-by-stage tower values) is equivalent to spectral decomposition data (bipolar channel coefficients): each determines the other via the canonical basis expansion.

name: Spectral-Germ Equivalence
summary: Spectral decomposition data is equivalent to germ data: knowing the spectral coefficients of a function at a point determines its omega-germ, and conversely.

name: Germ-Character Equivalence
summary: Omega-germ data at a point is equivalent to boundary character data on L: the germ determines a unique character via bipolar projection, and the character recovers the germ via idempotent assembly.

name: Character-Hartogs Equivalence
summary: Boundary character data is equivalent to Hartogs extension data: a character on L extends uniquely to a holomorphic function on tau^3 via the boundary lift, and every Hartogs extension restricts to a unique character.

name: Mutual Determination (5-Way Equivalence)
summary: Five equivalent descriptions of a tau-holomorphic function: (R) refinement sequence, (S) spectral decomposition, (G) omega-germ transformer, (C) boundary character, (H) Hartogs extension. The equivalences hold because each bipolar channel is one-dimensional.

name: Evolution Operator
summary: The composition of successive boundary lifts, propagating holomorphic data from stage n to stage m along the primorial tower.

name: Causal Arrow
summary: The preferred direction in the primorial tower selected by B/C asymmetry: stage n+1 is in the causal future of stage n, and the B-channel precedes the C-channel.

name: B/C Asymmetry Implies Time Arrow
summary: The B/C asymmetry in the bipolar decomposition of the evolution operator induces a preferred causal direction in the primorial tower: stage n+1 is in the causal future of stage n, and the B-channel precedes the C-channel.

name: Composition of Holomorphic Maps
summary: Composition of Holomorphic Maps

name: Identity Map
summary: The identity omega-germ transformer on a tau-admissible point x, defined by the identity map at each stage. The unit morphism in HolEnd(tau).

name: Associativity of Holomorphic Composition
summary: Composition of tau-holomorphic maps is associative: (h . g) . f = h . (g . f) for all composable holomorphic maps. This, together with the identity map, makes HolEnd(tau) a category.

name: Holomorphic Endomorphism Category
summary: Holomorphic Endomorphism Category

name: Laurent Expansion
summary: The bipolar double series expansion of an omega-germ transformer at a tau-admissible point, with coefficients in the canonical basis and idempotent projectors e_+, e_-.

name: Residue
summary: The split-complex residue extracted from the n = -1 Laurent coefficients. The bipolar residue decomposes into independent +/- channel contributions.

name: Meromorphic Function
summary: Meromorphic Function

name: Residue Theorem
summary: The spectral trace of a tau-meromorphic function with finitely many singularities equals the sum of its residues, with both sides decomposing along bipolar channels.

name: Canonical Holomorphic Basis
summary: Canonical Holomorphic Basis

name: Cylinder Generator
summary: The canonical indicator function at prime p and residue class v in channel sigma. The basis element for holomorphic functions on tau^3.

name: Finite Spectral Support
summary: Every holomorphic function on tau^3 has finite spectral support at each stage: only finitely many cylinder generators contribute to the canonical basis expansion at any given primorial level.

name: Projection Formula
summary: The spectral coefficient at prime p and residue class v is recovered by discrete Fourier average over the fiber, projected to the appropriate bipolar channel via the idempotent.

name: Holomorphic Presheaf
summary: Holomorphic Presheaf

name: Gluing Lemma
summary: Compatible tau-holomorphic sections on a cover by cylinder sub-domains glue uniquely to a global section, guaranteed by the ultrametric topology.

name: Sheaf Axioms
summary: The holomorphic presheaf O_tau on the cylinder topology is a sheaf: it satisfies locality and gluing for compatible local sections.

name: Canonical Decomposition
summary: Canonical Decomposition

name: Idempotent Decomposition Lemma
summary: Every holomorphic function f on tau^3 decomposes uniquely as f = e_+ f_+ + e_- f_- where f_+ and f_- are the B-channel and C-channel components. The decomposition is functorial, stage-coherent, and respects the canonical basis.

name: Decomposition Functoriality
summary: Decomposition Functoriality

name: Branch Factorization
summary: Branch Factorization

name: Prime-Split Support
summary: The branch factorization has canonically split spectral support: G_+ on B-channel primes and G_- on C-channel primes, with partition from Prime Polarity.

name: Polarity Symmetry
summary: The j-involution interchanges bipolar sectors: sigma_j(G_+) = G_- and sigma_j(G_-) = G_+, so one sector determines the full transformer.

name: Holomorphic iff Idempotent-Supported
summary: A tau-holomorphic function is holomorphic if and only if it is idempotent-supported: the bipolar projections e_+f and e_-f have canonically split spectral support on B-channel and C-channel primes respectively. Proved via the three-lemma chain (II.L08-L10).

name: Tau-Regularity
summary: A point p is tau-regular for f if the omega-germ sequence stabilizes at a finite primorial stage. A constructive, positive criterion: existence of stabilization, not absence of pathology.

name: Regularity Criterion
summary: A point p is tau-regular for f if and only if the omega-germ sequence stabilizes: there exists a finite stage N such that the boundary lift agrees with f for all stages beyond N. Constructive and decidable at each finite stage.

name: Positive vs Negative Regularity
summary: Positive vs Negative Regularity

name: Pre-Yoneda Embedding
summary: Pre-Yoneda Embedding

name: Functions as Tau-Objects
summary: Functions as Tau-Objects

name: Probe Naturality Equals Holomorphy
summary: Probe Naturality Equals Holomorphy

name: Code Map
summary: A pair of bipolar boundary coefficient streams recording spectral data at successive primorial stages. Encodes a holomorphic function via its boundary.

name: Decode Map
summary: The map reconstructing a tau-holomorphic function from a bipolar boundary coefficient stream via stage expansion, idempotent assembly, and limit.

name: Code/Decode Bijection
summary: Code and Decode are mutually inverse: a tau-holomorphic function IS its bipolar boundary coefficient stream, and every coherent bipolar stream IS a holomorphic function. The Holomorphic Content Theorem.

name: Diagonal Protection via K5
summary: Diagonal Protection via K5

name: Self-Enrichment Structure
summary: Self-Enrichment Structure

name: Hom Object
summary: The internal Hom object as the inverse limit of stage-k morphism sets. A tau-object with NF address, bipolar decomposition, and tower coherence.

name: Hom Bipolar Decomposition
summary: Hom Bipolar Decomposition

name: Probe Naturality iff Yoneda
summary: Probe Naturality iff Yoneda

name: Tau-Yoneda Embedding
summary: The Yoneda functor y: tau -> [tau^op, tau] is fully faithful: (1) well-defined with internal target via self-enrichment, (2) faithful by evaluation at id, (3) full via Code/Decode computable witnesses, (4) bipolar-preserving under the e_+/e_- decomposition.

name: 2-Category Structure
summary: 2-Category Structure

name: 2-Morphism
summary: A 2-cell between 1-cells in tau_2, defined as an element of the internal Hom-Hom. Well-defined because Hom objects are tau-objects via self-enrichment.

name: Enrichment Iteration
summary: Enrichment Iteration

name: E1 Preview
summary: Identifies the E_0 to E_1 transition within the four-level enrichment ladder (E_0 plain, E_1 self-enriched, E_2 spectral forces in Book III, E_3 physics/life/metaphysics in Books IV-VII).

name: E1 Enrichment Layer
summary: Category tau equipped with self-enrichment: internal Hom objects, Yoneda embedding, Code/Decode bijection, 2-category structure, and bipolar Hom decomposition.

name: Self-Description Property
summary: Self-Description Property

name: E0/E1 Transition
summary: The passage from E_0 (finite/profinite elements, external morphisms, algebraic bi-square) to E_1 (internal Hom, self-enrichment, Code/Decode, 2-category structure).

name: Enrichment Ladder Preview
summary: Enrichment Ladder Preview

name: Idempotent-Supported Character
summary: Idempotent-Supported Character

name: Character Algebra Ring Structure
summary: Character Algebra Ring Structure

name: Character Decomposition
summary: Every spectral character is idempotent-supported: there exist unique ring homomorphisms chi_+ and chi_- such that chi = e_+ chi_+ + e_- chi_-, with chi_+ determined by B-coordinate primes and chi_- by C-coordinate primes.

name: Extension in Split-Complex Codomain
summary: Extension in Split-Complex Codomain

name: Hartogs Extension Uniqueness
summary: Hartogs Extension Uniqueness

name: Stagewise Naturality
summary: Stagewise Naturality

name: Extensions Are Omega-Germ Transformers
summary: Hartogs extensions from L to tau^3 are the same as omega-germ transformers: the extension of boundary data through the tower produces precisely the tower-coherent family of stage-k morphisms that defines an omega-germ transformer.

name: Yoneda Application
summary: The Yoneda bijection identifies natural transformations with omega-germ transformers via a tower-coherent family of stage-k components.

name: Omega-Germs iff Holomorphic Functions
summary: The category of omega-germ transformers is equivalent to the category of tau-holomorphic functions: every omega-germ transformer arises from a unique holomorphic function, and conversely. Bridges the algebraic and analytic perspectives.

name: Spectral Algebra
summary: The algebra A_spec(L) of idempotent-supported characters on L = S^1 v S^1 valued in calibrated H_tau, with bipolar decomposition and ring structure from pointwise operations.

name: Central Theorem
summary: The Central Theorem: canonical isomorphism O(tau^3) = A_spec(L), identifying holomorphic functions on the fibered product with spectral characters on the lemniscate boundary. Functorial, bipolar-compatible, tower-graded, iota_tau-calibrated.

name: Holographic Principle
summary: The Central Theorem is an exact holographic correspondence: the 1-dimensional boundary data (characters on L) completely encodes the 3-dimensional interior data (holomorphic functions on tau^3). A proved theorem, not a conjecture.

name: Liouville Categorical Dodge
summary: The classical Liouville theorem does not apply to tau^3: j^2 = +1 gives a wave-type PDE operator (hyperbolic), not the elliptic Laplacian. The maximum principle fails, so non-constant bounded holomorphic functions exist.

name: Categoricity
summary: Any fibered product with an ABCD chart satisfying axioms K0-K5 is canonically isomorphic to tau^3, with the isomorphism unique and structure-preserving.

name: Moduli Space
summary: The set of isomorphism classes of fibered products satisfying axioms K0-K5. By categoricity (II.T42), the moduli space is a singleton.

name: Uniqueness of Category Tau
summary: The moduli space of tau^3-structures is a single point: any two realizations of axioms K0-K5 are canonically isomorphic, preserving the fibered product structure, ABCD chart, holomorphic structure O(tau^3) = A_spec(L), and calibration constants pi, e, j, iota_tau.

name: Complete Dependency Chain Audit
summary: Complete Dependency Chain Audit

name: Tau-Manifold
summary: A pair (M, A_tau) where M is a Stone space and A_tau is a maximal tau-analytic atlas. The model spaces are tau^1, T^2, and tau^3.

name: Tau-Analytic Atlas
summary: A collection of tau-analytic charts covering tau^3 with tower-coherent, stagewise natural, sector-independent transition functions.

name: Tau-Exterior Derivative
summary: Tau-Exterior Derivative

name: Tau3 Is a Tau-Manifold
summary: Tau3 Is a Tau-Manifold

name: BSD Bridge
summary: Identifies four layers required for categorical BSD: (1) Central Theorem, (2) proto-rationality, (3) tau-L-functions via spectral forces (Book III), (4) force-theoretic rank computation (Book III).

name: Proto-Rationality
summary: A holomorphic function whose spectral coefficients have finite support and lie in the canonical basis. The algebraic prerequisite for the BSD approach.

name: Spectral Forces Preview
summary: Spectral Forces Preview

name: E1 Export Package
summary: The complete collection of structures Book II delivers to Book III: categorical structure, topology, geometry, holomorphy, Central Theorem, enrichment, exterior derivative, and Code/Decode.

name: Open Questions and Research Agenda
summary: Open Questions and Research Agenda

name: Differential-Geometric Agenda
summary: Differential-Geometric Agenda

name: Enrichment Ladder Forward Declaration
summary: Enrichment Ladder Forward Declaration

name: Geometric Bi-Square
summary: The algebraic bi-square (I.T41) filled with geometric objects earned in Book II Parts I-IX: Stone topology on tau^3 (II.T07), continuous projections (II.T06), torus degeneration L = S^1 v S^1 (II.T13), calibrated H_tau (II.D35), spectral algebra A_spec(L) (II.D60). The pasted commuting diagram with earned geometric content.

name: Geometric Bi-Square Theorem
summary: Five properties of the geometric bi-square: (1) every algebraic component has geometric content, (2) all vertical arrows are continuous, (3) both squares commute with continuity, (4) the limit row is precisely the Central Theorem O(tau^3) = A_spec(L), (5) forgetting geometry recovers the algebraic bi-square I.T41.

name: Algebraic-to-Geometric Audit
summary: Algebraic skeleton (Book I) + geometric flesh (Book II) = geometric bi-square. Book I builds bottom-up (axioms -> algebra -> bi-square); Book II builds top-down (holomorphy -> continuity -> topology -> geometry). The two directions are compatible: geometric earning never contradicts algebraic structure.

name: Scaling Chain Forward
summary: The bi-square is the spine of the series: Level 0 (Book I) algebraic on finite cyclic groups, Level 1 (Book II) geometric with topology and Central Theorem, Level 2 (Book III) enriched with spectral forces and Langlands parallels. Six structural parallels: tower coherence <-> automorphic levels, spectral decomposition <-> Galois, CRT <-> Euler product, Global Hartogs <-> modularity, bi-square <-> functoriality, Mutual Determination <-> Master Schema.

name: Structural Sign Classification
summary: Twelve-level classification of structural outcomes forced by the scalar unit equation u^2 = +/-1, satisfying completeness, traceability (at most three steps from the sign), and monotonicity (downstream levels depend on upstream outcomes).

name: The Rewiring Thesis
summary: Every structural difference between orthodox complex analysis and tau-holomorphy traces, through at most three steps, to the single sign u^2 = +/-1. Verifiable and falsifiable structural claim grounded in the twelve-level classification.

name: The Infinity Trade-Off
summary: The pair of structural properties: (U) unique global omega with non-trivial spectrum, and (A) Archimedean local density with uncountably many elements in every neighborhood. The two properties represent the deepest structural divergence between orthodox and tau-mathematics.

name: Structural Incompatibility of Unique Omega and Archimedean Density
summary: Under K5, properties (U) unique global omega and (A) Archimedean local density cannot both hold. (A) implies not-(U) via the cardinality hierarchy; (U) + K5 implies not-(A) via the Inapplicability Theorem (I.T35) and Unique Infinity (I.T36).

name: What Each Path Buys
summary: Detailed comparison of what the epsilon-delta path buys (local resolution, Laplacian, conformal geometry, three centuries of analysis) versus the unique-omega path (canonical infinity, boundary-first paradigm, no cardinality paradoxes, finite-stage computation). Neither path dominates.

name: PDE Type Classification
summary: Dichotomy of holomorphic function theories by sign u^2 = +/-1: elliptic (Laplace, no characteristics, isotropic, max principle, symmetric boundary/interior, Liouville rigidity, miraculous Hartogs) vs hyperbolic (wave, two characteristic families, asymmetric, Liouville dodge, natural Hartogs).

name: Asymmetric Determination
summary: In tau-holomorphy, boundary data on L determines interior values on tau^3 asymmetrically: directional propagation along two characteristic families, no reverse constraint, Global Hartogs as wave front coverage, and uniqueness from sector independence.

name: Why Hartogs Is the Wave Front
summary: Global Hartogs extension is not a theorem about extending functions but about wave propagation. In the elliptic world it is a theorem of analysis (d-bar theory); in the hyperbolic world it is a theorem of geometry (wave fronts from L cover the entire interior tau^3).

name: Stage-Finite Euclidean Geometry
summary: Geometric structure of tau^3 determined by stage spaces Z/P_k Z (finite cyclic groups with positive-definite cyclic metric), stage-k Tarski axioms, and profinite limit preserving Euclidean geometry. Discretely Euclidean at every finite stage, profinitely Euclidean in the limit.

name: Parallel Preservation
summary: The parallel postulate (II.T18) survives the hyperbolic algebraic substrate j^2 = +1: stage-k geometry is Euclidean, Tarski axioms are universal sentences preserved under inverse limits, and light cones require tangent spaces that tau^3 does not possess.

name: The Minkowski Comparison
summary: Minkowski and tau share j^2 = +1 but differ in substrate: Minkowski on continuous manifold produces light cones and destroyed parallels; tau on profinite space produces no light cones and Euclidean geometry. Same algebra, different substrate, different geometry.

name: The Rewiring Table
summary: Twelve-level structural comparison between orthodox complex analysis (i^2 = -1) and tau-holomorphy (j^2 = +1). Levels: scalar algebra, holomorphy PDE, boundary-interior, infinity, cardinality, topology, geometry, compactness, idempotents, Liouville, gluing, spectrum.

name: Honest Accounting
summary: Honest accounting of structural costs: orthodox pays ZFC imports, multiple infinities, non-constructive existence, imported topology. Tau pays no epsilon-delta, no local smoothness, no classical Laplacian, zero divisors. Neither side is free.

name: Spectral Forces Inherit Hyperbolicity
summary: All eight spectral forces are wave-type operators on O(tau^3) with bipolar decomposition (H1), directional propagation along characteristics (H2), and iota_tau calibration (H3). Classical problems (RH, Navier-Stokes, Langlands) are elliptic shadows of hyperbolic structures.

name: The Physics Quadrant Matrix
summary: 2x2 classification of mathematical frameworks by PDE type (elliptic/hyperbolic) and metric type (Archimedean/non-Archimedean). QFT in (H,A), GR locally in (E,A), Category tau in (H,nA). The fourth quadrant (H,nA) is the only quadrant where the Unification Obstruction does not apply.

name: The Unification Obstruction
summary: Structural incompatibility within the Archimedean column: no framework can simultaneously support hyperbolic propagation (wave-type PDE with characteristics and causal ordering) and elliptic regularity (Laplacian-type with isotropic diffusion and smoothing) at the same point at all energy scales.

name: Fourth Quadrant Resolution
summary: In the (Hyperbolic, non-Archimedean) quadrant of Category tau: wave characteristics exist (R1), no Laplacian infinities (R2), stage-finite Euclidean geometry (R3), discrete exterior derivative d_tau (R4), bipolar quantum phases from e_+/e_- (R5), and the Unification Obstruction does not apply (R6).

name: Why Every Unification Attempt Fails
summary: String theory, loop quantum gravity, causal dynamical triangulations, and asymptotic safety all remain in the Archimedean column and encounter the Unification Obstruction: none can simultaneously support hyperbolic propagation and elliptic regularity in a self-consistent manner at all scales.

name: Books IV-V Preview
summary: Preview of Books IV-V physics programme: fiber T^2 carries QM (Book IV), base tau^1 carries GR (Book V), fibration tau^3 = tau^1 x_f T^2 is itself the unification. The Central Theorem ensures holomorphic functions on tau^3 are determined by boundary characters.

name: Archimedean-Elliptic Engine
summary: The mechanism generating the orthodox SCV dimensional ladder: interaction of Archimedean metric dimension (continuous manifold with free parameter n) and elliptic CR overdeterminacy (n(n-1)/2 integrability conditions beyond n basic equations). Requires BOTH ingredients; neither alone generates a ladder.

name: Dimensional Rigidity
summary: tau admits exactly one holomorphic structure with fibration index 3 (= 1 base + 2 fiber) and refinement dimension 4 (= ABCD rays). There is no family of tau-structures indexed by dimension; the 3 in tau^3 is an algebraic fibration index, not a metric dimension parameter.

name: Simultaneous Rung Theorem
summary: tau^3 simultaneously exhibits features from at least three classical SCV dimension rungs: full Hartogs extension (C3 feature via I.T31), distinguished boundary (C2 feature via torus degeneration II.T13), and complete boundary determination (C1 feature via Mutual Determination II.T27), while lacking Riemann mapping, monodromy, isolated singularities, and the Levi problem.

name: Fourth Quadrant Ladder Collapse
summary: In the (Hyperbolic, Non-Archimedean) quadrant the Archimedean-elliptic engine is absent: no continuous metric dimension (LC1), no progressive elliptic overdeterminacy (LC2), therefore no dimensional ladder (LC3), and features from multiple classical rungs coexist (LC4).

name: Categoricity Implies No Ladder
summary: The categoricity of tau^3 (II.T42, moduli = singleton) independently forces no dimensional ladder: a singleton moduli space cannot support a dimension-indexed family of holomorphic structures.

name: Honest Scope: Ladder vs. SCV
summary: Honest scope declaration: the dimensional ladder collapse is a structural observation about why features coexist, not a claim that tau^3 reproduces all classical SCV content. SCV remains the deepest theory of holomorphic functions in several variables; the claim is that the qualitative stratification by complex dimension does not arise in the (Hyperbolic, Non-Archimedean) quadrant.

name: τ-Connection
summary: A connection on the primorial tower: a transport operator Γ_k(x,v) = (x+v) mod M_k at each stage, satisfying tower compatibility. The canonical flat connection uses additive transport.

name: Parallel Transport
summary: Parallel transport along a path γ in Z/M_k Z: sequential composition of the connection transport operator along each edge. For the flat connection, this reduces to addition mod M_k.

name: Flat Connection Existence
summary: The additive connection Γ_k(x,v) = (x+v) mod M_k is flat: parallel transport around any closed loop returns to the starting point. Verified computationally at stages 1-2.

name: Holonomy Triviality
summary: At each finite stage, the holonomy group of the flat connection is trivial. Z/M_k Z is simply connected as a discrete set, so all loops are contractible. The profinite limit may acquire nontrivial holonomy from π₁(L) = Z.

name: τ-Curvature
summary: Curvature R(v,w)(x) = Γ(Γ(x,v),w) - Γ(Γ(x,w),v) measures parallel transport non-commutativity. For the flat connection on Z/M_k Z, R = 0.

name: τ-Geodesic
summary: Geodesic in Z/M_k Z: shortest path in the cyclic distance. The geodesic direction from x to y is the minimal displacement mod M_k.

name: Flat Curvature Vanishing
summary: The canonical flat connection has zero curvature at every finite stage. R = 0 because addition in Z/M_k Z is commutative. Verified at stages 1-2.

name: Geodesic Completeness
summary: Every geodesic in Z/M_k Z can be extended indefinitely: the space is compact and complete at every finite stage.

name: Lemniscate Holonomy
summary: The profinite limit acquires nontrivial holonomy from π₁(L) ≅ ℤ. The generator maps to the unit shift x ↦ x+1 mod M_k, which has order M_k at stage k.

name: L² Inner Product
summary: L² inner product on Z/M_k Z: ⟨f,g⟩_k = (1/M_k) Σ f(x)g(x). Represented as rational pair. Symmetric, bilinear, with orthogonal indicator basis.

name: L² Norm
summary: L² norm squared: ‖f‖²_k = ⟨f,f⟩_k. Non-negative by construction (sum of squares). Zero iff f = 0 (definiteness).

name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
summary: Cauchy-Schwarz inequality |⟨f,g⟩|² ≤ ‖f‖²·‖g‖². Verified exhaustively for indicator basis functions at stages 1-2.

name: L² Completeness
summary: L² space at each finite stage is automatically complete (finite-dimensional). Tower compatibility ensures the inverse system of L² spaces is coherent.

name: Chain Complex
summary: Chain complex on the primorial tower. The boundary map d_n reduces from stage n to stage n-1. Tower coherence: reduce(reduce(x,n-1),n-2) = reduce(x,n-2).

name: Homology via SES
summary: Homology via short exact sequences: 0 → Z/M_{k-1} → Z/M_k → Z/p_k → 0. Exactness at the middle: ker(g) = im(f), giving trivial homology H = 0.

name: Tower Coherence
summary: Boundary maps compose coherently: d_{n-1}(d_n(x)) = d_{n-1}(x) for the primorial chain. This is tower coherence of the inverse system.

name: Long Exact Sequence
summary: Short exact sequence of primorial quotients induces a long exact sequence in homology via the snake lemma. Connecting homomorphism δ exists.

name: Čech Complex
summary: Čech complex for the cylinder cover of Z/M_k Z. Coboundary δ⁰(f)(a,b) = f(b) - f(a). Satisfies δ² = 0 (alternating sum identity).

name: Sheaf Cohomology Groups
summary: Sheaf cohomology on the primorial tower. H⁰ = global sections (rank 1 for constant sheaf). H¹ = 0 (ultrametric cover is acyclic).

name: H⁰ = Global Sections
summary: H⁰ equals the space of global sections. For the constant sheaf, a 0-cochain is a global section iff it is constant. Non-constant functions have nonzero coboundary.

name: Čech-to-Derived Comparison
summary: Čech cohomology agrees with derived functor cohomology on the primorial tower. The cylinder cover is acyclic (ultrametric: intersections are empty or cylinders).

name: Denotation Map as Shadow Functor
summary: The denotation map Den: tau -> Mfd_sm factors as a shadow functor Sh that discards finite-stage structure while preserving smooth geometry. Connects orthodox bridge to dimensional ladder.

name: HolEnd Exponentials
summary: The holomorphic endomorphism category HolEnd(tau) admits internal exponentials: [A x B, C] = [A, [B, C]] for all objects A, B, C in HolEnd(tau). Follows from self-enrichment closure.

name: Tau-Exponential
summary: The tau-exponential E_tau: HolEnd(tau) -> HolEnd(tau) sends A to [A, A]. It is sigma-equivariant: E_tau(sigma(A)) = sigma(E_tau(A)) for the involution sigma.

name: Tau-Exponential Sigma-Equivariance
summary: Sigma-equivariance of E_tau reflects that self-interaction respects the bipolar involution. The fixed-point locus E_tau^sigma recovers the diagonal of HolEnd.

name: Shadow Families in SCV
summary: Tower families in the dimensional ladder project to shadow families via the shadow functor. These projections preserve cohomological data while forgetting finite-stage detail, connecting SCV collapse to classical manifold geometry.

name: Five Comparison Modes
summary: Five exhaustive, mutually exclusive comparison modes (A: Same, B: Parallel, C: Refused, D: Gained, E: Earned) for classifying every tau-construction against its orthodox counterpart.

name: Six Comparison Axes
summary: Six structural axes along which Category tau and orthodox mathematics differ: infinity, sign, topology, geometry, analysis, and category theory.

name: Mode C Catalog
summary: Catalog of 16 Mode C (Refused) constructions: orthodox structures that Category tau structurally cannot support, including algebraic closure, conformal geometry, Hilbert spaces, and continuous spectra.

name: Mode E Catalog
summary: Catalog of 13 Mode E (Earned) results: constructions that both frameworks prove, but tau derives from fewer axioms. Includes induction, Euclidean geometry, number tower, and topos structure.

name: Combined Gain-Cost Ledger
summary: Combined gain-cost ledger: 49 gains (23 Mode D + 13 Mode E + 17 neutral) vs 16 Mode C costs. No partial selection possible due to trade-off atomicity.

name: Trade-Off Atomicity
summary: The gain-cost structure of Category tau is atomic: accepting K0-K6 simultaneously forces all 16 Mode C costs and enables all Mode D gains and Mode E earned results. No partial selection possible.

name: Protection Architecture
summary: The 16 Mode C refusals form a coherent protection architecture: three interlocking barriers (K6 object closure, K5 diagonal discipline, j^2=+1 forced sign) that protect categoricity, decidability, and finite witnesses.

name: Epistemic Upgrade Principle
summary: Mode E results obey the epistemic upgrade principle: if two frameworks prove the same theorem but one uses fewer axioms, that framework provides a strictly stronger explanation.

name: The Reality Test
summary: The reality test: whether the fork is worth it depends on whether the tau path leads to a viable description of physics. Books IV-V will test this.

name: Bridge Declaration
summary: Bridge declaration: the fork between Category tau and orthodox mathematics is structural and permanent, but the two sides can inform each other through shadow functors and comparison principles.
